key_insight: AITutor shifts AI math help from giving answers to supporting reasoning repair, because students under exam pressure use shortcut checks as a practical way to diagnose mistakes.
engineering_relevance: For AI tutor builders, the paper shows that layered examples, step-linked visuals, and metacognitive prompts can be designed as low-friction interface mechanisms that support mathematical reasoning in high-stakes settings.
method: Generative study to translate pedagogical theory into interface features
Usability study of the AITutor system
evidence_signal: The evaluation combines 7,379 telemetry events, 8 contextual observations, and 10 interviews with junior-high students preparing for Zhongkao exams.
watch_item: The findings are context-specific to high-stakes exam preparation, so systems optimized for guided reasoning may still need careful tuning to avoid increasing cognitive load or slowing urgent problem solving.
paper_title: From Answer Generators to Reasoning Facilitators: Designing AI Tutors for Mathematical Reasoning in High-Stakes Environments
